questions.text: What is the primary advantage of using Plan Mode in Claude Code before implementing a feature?

options.text: It makes Claude Code run faster
It separates research and planning from execution, preventing solving the wrong problem
It automatically generates test cases
It locks files to prevent accidental edits

questions.text: When working with Claude Code, what is the most effective way to verify UI changes?

options.text: Ask Claude to describe what the page looks like
Manually open the browser every time
Provide screenshots, tests, or expected outputs so Claude can check its own work
Trust that Claude's output is always correct

questions.text: What happens when Claude Code's context window fills up during a long session?

options.text: Claude automatically saves to disk and continues
Performance degrades — Claude may forget earlier instructions or make more mistakes
The session crashes and you lose all work
Claude switches to a smaller model automatically

questions.text: What is the recommended approach when Claude Code makes the same mistake twice despite corrections?

options.text: Keep correcting until it gets it right
Add more detailed instructions to CLAUDE.md
Run /clear and start a fresh session with a better initial prompt incorporating what you learned
Switch to a different AI model

questions.text: In the context of AI-assisted development, what does 'Discernment' in the 4D framework refer to?

options.text: The ability to write code without any AI assistance
Critically evaluating AI suggestions — asking why, comparing approaches, and rejecting bad outputs
Delegating all coding tasks to the AI
Describing problems in great technical detail
